concept_name: Potential Energy in an External Field — U = qV (a charge), U = q₁V₁+q₂V₂ (a system), U = −p·E (a dipole)
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.2 Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance (§2.8 Potential energy in an external field) — table-of-contents + Indian-anchor reference only; teaching authored from first principles. The atomic idea: the potential energy of a charge in a GIVEN external field is U = qV, where V is the potential made by sources somewhere else — the placed charge SAMPLES the field, it does not create the V it sits in. For two charges it is U = q₁V₁ + q₂V₂ (each charge's own qV in the SAME external field), and for a dipole the two qV terms collapse into one clean formula U = −p·E = −pE·cosθ. U carries the sign of q·V: a like sign stores +U (a hill), an opposite sign gives −U (a well). The dipole energy is a MINIMUM at θ=0° (aligned, stable) and a MAXIMUM at θ=180° (anti-aligned, unstable); the torque τ = pE·sinθ is a DIFFERENT quantity that peaks at θ=90°. SCOPE: this is the external-field ENERGY diamond of Ch.2. It declares electric_potential_meaning, electric_potential_point_charge, potential_energy_system_of_charges and electric_dipole_in_field as prerequisites and does NOT re-teach them. It is DISTINCT from the mutual PE kq₁q₂/r of §2.7 (contrasted in STATE_4) and does NOT cover torque dynamics (electric_dipole_in_field, referenced STATE_6) or capacitor energy ½CV². field_3d scenario_type pe_external_field (already built): a CHARGE phase (STATE_1..4) where charges park at points of GIVEN potential V, each carrying a signed U=qV number tag while a signed energy meter sums them and the equipotential landscape shades the V; and a DIPOLE phase (STATE_5..7) reusing the dipole-in-field engine — p arrow, θ arc, U=−pE·cosθ meter, a damped-pendulum release from θ≈180° down to 0°, plus a q + θ slider in STATE_7 (energy_explorer). Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches; misconceptions confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch + a straightforward contrast beat — no predict-pause, per Rule 31). RECONSTRUCTED 2026-07-08 (9→7 states) for Rule 31/32 compliance: STATE_2+STATE_3 merged (single-charge sign-of-U beat, PRIMARY aha), STATE_7+STATE_8 merged (energy extrema + stability shown via one damped-pendulum release instead of two separate beats).

real_world_anchor.primary: Think of gel electrophoresis in a biology or forensics lab. Negatively charged DNA fragments are dropped into a gel with a voltage across it, and they drift toward the positive electrode — sliding DOWN their U = qV energy landscape precisely because their charge q is negative. The field and the potential V are set by the electrodes; the DNA only samples that landscape and rolls to lower energy.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture the electron gun in an old CRT television tube. An electron is accelerated across a voltage V and arrives at the screen with kinetic energy equal to the magnitude of its charge times V — it has fallen through the U = qV landscape, trading potential energy for speed, exactly the way a ball rolls downhill.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Picture a single pixel in an LCD screen. Tiny rod-shaped polar molecules behave like dipoles; in the applied field they rotate to their lowest-energy orientation — the aligned U = −p·E minimum — twisting the light passing through on or off.

q.name: Signed test charge placed in the external field (STATE_2 demo value; live slider in STATE_7)

V1.name: GIVEN external potential at the q1 location

V2.name: GIVEN external potential at the q2 location

V_single.name: GIVEN external potential at the STATE_2 single-charge demo point — a fixed backdrop value, NOT a slider (reinforces 'the field/V is given, not adjustable')

d.name: Charge separation in the dipole (p = q·d)

theta_deg.name: Angle from the dipole moment p to the field E

formulas.single_charge: U = qV — energy of charge q at a point where the EXTERNAL field holds potential V; V is the environment, q only samples it.
formulas.delta_U: ΔU = qΔV — moving to higher V raises U for +q, lowers it for −q.
formulas.two_charges: U = q₁V₁ + q₂V₂ — each charge contributes its own qV in the SAME external field; NOT the mutual kq₁q₂/r.
formulas.dipole_collapse: U = (+q)V₊ + (−q)V₋ = q(V₊−V₋); with V=−E·r, V₊−V₋=−E·d, so U = −(qd)·E = −p·E = −pE·cosθ.
formulas.dipole_extrema: U_min=−pE at θ=0° (stable); U=0 at θ=90°; U_max=+pE at θ=180° (unstable).
formulas.torque_aside: τ = pE·sinθ is a DIFFERENT quantity (peaks at θ=90°). Full torque: electric_dipole_in_field.

constraints: V and E are GIVEN by the external source — independent of the placed charge; the charge samples the field, it does not create the V it sits in (C1, the M1 confront).
External-field PE U=qV (or ΣqᵢVᵢ) is DISTINCT from mutual PE kq₁q₂/r — never add a mutual term into U_system (C2).
The sign of U is the sign of q·V: like signs store +U (hill); opposite signs give −U (well) (C3).
Dipole energy U=−p·E is defined up to a constant; reference U=0 at θ=90° (C4).
θ measured from p to E; U minimum at θ=0° (stable), maximum at θ=180° (unstable) (C5).
V_single (STATE_2's fixed demo point, =4) is DISTINCT from V1 (the system's first-charge location, =5, STATE_3/4) — do not conflate the two (C6).
All on-screen energies use round DEMO units (pE=U_FULL=12), not SI (C7).

aha_moment.statement: A charge's energy can be negative — the field's hill becomes a well.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: STATE_2: the signed meter dips to −8 for the negative charge parked at V=4; the narration confirms a positive charge there would read +8 instead — same spot, opposite sign.

tts_sentences.text_en: Before any charge arrives, an external field E already fills this space, and with it a potential V — high ground, low ground, set by sources somewhere else.
tts_sentences.text_hi: किसी भी charge के arrive करने से पहले, एक external field E इस space को भरता है, और इसके साथ एक potential V — high ground, low ground, कहीं और के sources द्वारा set किया हुआ।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఏ charge అయినా arrive కాకముందే, ఒక external field E ఈ space ని నింపుతుంది, మరియు దానితో potential V — high ground, low ground, వేరే sources ద్వారా set చేయబడింది.

tts_sentences.text_en: This is the stage.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह stage है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇది stage.

STATE_2.title: U = qV: a negative charge sinks into a well

scene_composition.text: q < 0 in a V > 0 region ⇒ U < 0

tts_sentences.text_en: A charge q, negative two, settles where the potential is four: U equals q V, minus eight — the meter dips below zero, a well of energy.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक charge q, minus two, जहाँ potential चार है वहाँ settle होता है: U equals q V, minus eight — meter zero से नीचे चला जाता है, energy का एक well।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక charge q, minus two, potential నాలుగు ఉన్నచోట settle అవుతుంది: U equals q V, minus eight — meter zero కిందకు వెళ్తుంది, energy యొక్క well.

tts_sentences.text_en: A positive charge here would read plus eight instead, a hill.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ एक positive charge plus eight पढ़ेगी, एक hill।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ positive charge plus eight చదువుతుంది, ఒక hill.

tts_sentences.text_en: Energy is not always positive.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Energy हमेशा positive नहीं होती।
tts_sentences.text_te: Energy ఎల్లప్పుడూ positive కాదు.

misconception_watch.belief: Potential energy of a charge is always positive.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The signed meter reads −8 for the negative charge parked at V=4; a positive charge at that same spot would read +8.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: U carries the sign of q·V — a negative charge in a +V region has negative U, a well not a hill.

STATE_3.title: Second charge arrives — energies simply add

tts_sentences.text_en: A second charge arrives: q two, minus one, at potential four — contributing minus four.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दूसरा charge arrive होता है: q two, minus one, potential चार पर — minus four contribute करता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండవ charge arrive అవుతుంది: q two, minus one, potential నాలుగు వద్ద — minus four contribute చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: With the first charge's plus ten already there, the energies simply add: U equals q one V one plus q two V two, plus six.
tts_sentences.text_hi: पहले charge का plus ten पहले से मौजूद है, energies simply add होती हैं: U equals q one V one plus q two V two, plus six।
tts_sentences.text_te: మొదటి charge యొక్క plus ten ఇప్పటికే ఉంది, energies simply add అవుతాయి: U equals q one V one plus q two V two, plus six.

STATE_4.title: External qV is not the mutual energy

tts_sentences.text_en: This energy is each charge sitting in the external field — q V bonds reach outward to the field, never to each other.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यह energy हर charge का external field में बैठना है — q V bonds field तक outward जाते हैं, कभी एक दूसरे तक नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఈ energy ప్రతి charge external field లో కూర్చోవడం — q V bonds field వైపు outward వెళ్తాయి, ఎప్పుడూ ఒకదానికొకటి కాదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: That charge-to-charge bond, k q one q two over r, is a different quantity.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वो charge-to-charge bond, k q one q two over r, एक अलग quantity है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆ charge-to-charge bond, k q one q two over r, వేరే quantity.

tts_sentences.text_en: Count only q V here; never add the mutual term in.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ सिर्फ q V गिनें; mutual term को कभी add न करें।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ కేవలం q V మాత్రమే count చేయండి; mutual term ను ఎప్పుడూ add చేయవద్దు.

misconception_watch.visual_counter: Two distinct bond types are shown simultaneously and never merge: charge→field qV bonds versus a separate charge↔charge kq₁q₂/r bond.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: qV is the charge + EXTERNAL-field energy; kq₁q₂/r is the charges' mutual energy — never add it in here.

STATE_5.title: Two qV terms collapse into U = −p·E

tts_sentences.text_en: A plus q and a minus q, a fixed distance d apart — a dipole, moment p pointing from minus to plus.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक plus q और एक minus q, एक fixed distance d पर — एक dipole, moment p pointing from minus to plus।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక plus q మరియు ఒక minus q, ఒక fixed distance d దూరంలో — ఒక dipole, moment p pointing from minus to plus.

tts_sentences.text_en: Its energy is just the two q V terms, which collapse into one clean formula: U equals minus p dot E, equals minus p E cosine theta.
tts_sentences.text_hi: इसकी energy बस दो q V terms हैं, जो एक clean formula में collapse हो जाते हैं: U equals minus p dot E, equals minus p E cosine theta।
tts_sentences.text_te: దీని energy కేవలం రెండు q V terms, అవి ఒక clean formula లో collapse అవుతాయి: U equals minus p dot E, equals minus p E cosine theta.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here that's minus six.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ वह minus six है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ అది minus six.

STATE_6.title: Aligned looks stable — it's actually the maximum

tts_sentences.text_en: Start anti-aligned near theta one eighty: torque is zero, energy at its maximum, plus p E — it looks stable.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Start करें anti-aligned near theta one eighty: torque है zero, energy अपने maximum पर, plus p E — यह stable लगता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Start చేయండి anti-aligned near theta one eighty: torque zero, energy దాని maximum వద్ద, plus p E — ఇది stable గా కనిపిస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Release it: the dipole lingers, then swings down through ninety, the meter crossing zero, and settles at theta zero — the true stable minimum, minus p E.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Release करें: dipole lingers करता है, फिर swings down होता है ninety के through, meter crossing zero, और settle होता है theta zero पर — true stable minimum, minus p E।
tts_sentences.text_te: Release చేయండి: dipole lingers అవుతుంది, తర్వాత swings down అవుతుంది ninety ద్వారా, meter crossing zero, మరియు settle అవుతుంది theta zero వద్ద — true stable minimum, minus p E.

tts_sentences.text_en: Zero torque was never enough.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Zero torque कभी enough नहीं था।
tts_sentences.text_te: Zero torque ఎప్పుడూ enough కాదు.

misconception_watch.belief: Aligned means maximum energy, and both zero-torque poses are equally stable.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The meter is pinned at the TOP at θ=178°, then the release physically carries it to the BOTTOM at θ=0°.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: θ=0° is the stable MINIMUM; θ=180° is the unstable MAXIMUM; torque peaks at 90° — a different quantity.

STATE_7.title: Your turn — qV mode and −p·E mode

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब आपकी बारी।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు మీ వంతు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Slide charge q — flip its sign, change its size — and watch U equals q V swing positive or negative.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Slide करें charge q — इसका sign flip करें, इसका size change करें — और देखें U equals q V कैसे swing करता है positive या negative।
tts_sentences.text_te: Slide చేయండి charge q — దాని sign flip చేయండి, దాని size change చేయండి — మరియు చూడండి U equals q V ఎలా swing అవుతుంది positive లేదా negative.

tts_sentences.text_en: Then sweep the angle theta and watch U equals minus p E cosine theta dip to its minimum at zero and rise to its maximum at one eighty.
tts_sentences.text_hi: फिर sweep करें angle theta और देखें U equals minus p E cosine theta कैसे dip करता है अपने minimum पर zero और rise करता है अपने maximum पर one eighty।
tts_sentences.text_te: తర్వాత sweep చేయండి angle theta మరియు చూడండి U equals minus p E cosine theta ఎలా dip అవుతుంది దాని minimum వద్ద zero మరియు rise అవుతుంది దాని maximum వద్ద one eighty.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same idea both times: two q V terms, collapsed or not.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Same idea दोनों बार: दो q V terms, collapsed हों या न हों।
tts_sentences.text_te: Same idea రెండు సార్లు: రెండు q V terms, collapsed అయినా కాకపోయినా.

assessment.mastery_definition: A student has mastered the potential energy of a charge in an external field when they can (1) write U = qV for a single charge sampling a GIVEN external potential and treat V/E as the environment, not as something the placed charge makes; (2) reject the 'energy is always positive' belief and assign U the sign of q·V — a hill for like signs, a well for opposite; (3) write the two-charge system energy as U = q₁V₁ + q₂V₂, each charge in the same external field; (4) distinguish this external-field PE from the mutual PE kq₁q₂/r between the charges and never add the mutual term; (5) derive U = −p·E = −pE·cosθ as the two qV terms collapsed, and reason that U is minimum at θ = 0° (stable) and maximum at θ = 180° (unstable); and (6) separate the energy extrema (0°/180°) from the torque peak (90°).

questions.stem: A charge q is placed at a point where an EXTERNAL field already holds a potential V. What is its potential energy?

options.A: U = qV — the charge samples the external potential V
options.B: U = kq/V — energy is charge over potential
options.C: U = qE — energy is charge times field, not potential
options.D: U = 0 — a single charge in an external field stores no energy

distractor_misconceptions.B: invents a q/V form; the relation is the product qV
distractor_misconceptions.C: confuses potential V with field E — qE has units of force not energy
distractor_misconceptions.D: thinks one charge in an external field has no energy, but U=qV is nonzero where V≠0

questions.tested_idea: The energy of a charge in a given external field is U = qV.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A test charge sits at a location whose external potential is V. Write its potential energy in terms of q and V.

questions.stem: A NEGATIVE charge (q < 0) is placed at a point where the external potential V is positive. What is the sign of its potential energy U?

options.A: Negative — U = qV with q<0 and V>0 gives U<0 (an energy well)
options.B: Positive — potential energy of a charge is always positive
options.C: Zero — the negative charge cancels the positive potential
options.D: Positive — a negative charge always raises the energy

distractor_misconceptions.B: holds the 'energy is always positive' belief, ignoring that U carries the sign of q·V
distractor_misconceptions.C: imagines q and V cancelling to zero, but U=qV is a product not a difference
distractor_misconceptions.D: assumes a negative charge raises U, but with positive V it lowers U below zero

questions.tested_idea: U = qV takes the sign of q·V; a negative charge in positive potential gives negative U.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A positive charge is placed where the external potential is NEGATIVE. State and justify the sign of its potential energy.

questions.stem: Two charges q₁ and q₂ sit at points of external potential V₁ and V₂. What is the total potential energy in that external field?

options.A: U = q₁V₁ + q₂V₂ — each charge contributes its own qV
options.B: U = (q₁ + q₂)(V₁ + V₂) — multiply total charge by total potential
options.C: U = q₁V₂ + q₂V₁ — cross-pair the charges and potentials
options.D: U = k q₁q₂ / r₁₂ — the energy between the two charges

distractor_misconceptions.B: lumps charges and potentials into one product instead of summing each charge's own qV
distractor_misconceptions.C: pairs each charge with the OTHER charge's potential, but each charge sits in the potential AT ITS OWN location
distractor_misconceptions.D: gives the MUTUAL energy between the charges, not their energy in the EXTERNAL field

questions.tested_idea: The external-field energy of several charges is the sum of each charge's own qV.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Three charges q₁, q₂, q₃ sit where the external potentials are V₁, V₂, V₃. Write the total potential energy in that external field.

questions.stem: For two charges in an external field, how does the external-field energy U = q₁V₁ + q₂V₂ relate to the mutual energy kq₁q₂/r₁₂ between the charges?

options.A: They are different quantities — qV uses the external potential; kq₁q₂/r is the charges' energy with each OTHER. You do not add the mutual term into U here
options.B: They are the same thing written two ways
options.C: You must always add kq₁q₂/r₁₂ to q₁V₁ + q₂V₂ to get the external-field energy
options.D: The external potential V is created by the two charges themselves

distractor_misconceptions.B: conflates external-field PE with mutual PE — they answer different questions
distractor_misconceptions.C: wrongly folds the mutual term into the external-field energy
distractor_misconceptions.D: holds the core M1 misconception that the placed charges make the external V

questions.tested_idea: External-field PE (qV) is distinct from mutual PE (kq₁q₂/r); the external V is given, not made by the placed charges.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Explain why the energy of a charge sitting in a capacitor's field (qV) is not the same calculation as the energy between that charge and another nearby charge.

questions.stem: A dipole of moment p sits in a uniform field E at angle θ. Its energy is U = −pE·cosθ. At which angle is U a MINIMUM?

options.A: θ = 0° (p aligned with E), where U = −pE
options.C: θ = 90° (p perpendicular to E), where U = 0
options.D: U is the same at every angle

distractor_misconceptions.B: picks anti-alignment, which is actually the energy MAXIMUM (+pE)
distractor_misconceptions.C: picks perpendicular, where U=0 — that is where the TORQUE peaks, not where U is least

questions.tested_idea: U = −pE·cosθ is minimum (−pE) at θ = 0° — alignment is the lowest-energy pose.
questions.parallel_form_stem: Using U = −pE·cosθ, state the angle at which a dipole in a uniform field has its HIGHEST potential energy, and give that energy.

questions.stem: A dipole rests aligned with the field (θ = 0°) and another rests anti-aligned (θ = 180°). How do the two equilibria differ?

options.A: θ = 0° is stable (U minimum, a nudge restores it); θ = 180° is unstable (U maximum, a nudge flips it to 0°)
options.B: Both are equally stable, since the torque is zero at both
options.C: θ = 180° is stable and θ = 0° is unstable
options.D: Neither is an equilibrium, because the field keeps turning the dipole

distractor_misconceptions.B: equates zero torque with stability, ignoring the energy criterion (min vs max)
distractor_misconceptions.D: denies that the zero-torque orientations are equilibria at all

questions.tested_idea: U = −pE·cosθ: θ = 0° is the stable minimum, θ = 180° the unstable maximum.
questions.parallel_form_stem: A dipole is released slightly displaced from θ = 180°. Describe what happens and explain it using the energy U = −pE·cosθ.

STATE_2.label: U = qV: a negative charge sinks into a well
STATE_2.caption: A negative charge, a well

STATE_3.label: Second charge arrives — energies simply add
STATE_3.caption: Second charge, energies add

STATE_4.label: External-field PE is NOT mutual PE
STATE_4.caption: qV is not mutual

STATE_6.label: Aligned looks stable — it's actually the maximum
STATE_6.caption: Zero torque isn't stable

STATE_7.label: Your turn — qV mode and −p·E mode
STATE_7.caption: All yours
